CKSPEC-AGENT-001.evidence: AGENTS.md exists (358+ lines). Covers project purpose, directory structure, all task commands, coding conventions, ADR lookup, testing strategy, troubleshooting.

CKSPEC-AGENT-002.evidence: AGENTS.md contains no Claude/Gemini/Copilot-specific instructions; provider content lives in CLAUDE.md, .cursorrules and copilot-instructions.md. A grep check rejects provider names (Claude, Gemini, Copilot, ChatGPT, Anthropic, OpenAI, Cursor) in AGENTS.md except on lines that reference the provider-specific files themselves (file-tree entries and cross-references), so a provider instruction such as 'Claude should ...' fails conformance.
CKSPEC-AGENT-002.violation_evidence: Residual gap (reviewed, accepted): the grep filters whole lines that mention a provider FILE, so a provider instruction sharing a line with such a cross-reference (e.g. 'Claude must X, see CLAUDE.md') escapes it — those lines remain a review concern. TestViolation_AGENT002_ProviderInstructionCaught proves the check passes on the current AGENTS.md and catches a plain provider-instruction line, running the exact command from this mapping against a doctored copy.

CKSPEC-AGENT-003.evidence: CLAUDE.md references AGENTS.md first. No project knowledge duplicated. .cursorrules and copilot-instructions.md also reference AGENTS.md.
CKSPEC-AGENT-003.violation_evidence: If a provider guide stops referencing AGENTS.md, the grep check fails.

CKSPEC-AGENT-004.evidence: AGENTS.md is self-sufficient: project purpose, directory tree, all task commands, coding conventions, ADR table, git workflow, troubleshooting with cascading fix order.
CKSPEC-AGENT-004.violation_evidence: Removing the required sections from AGENTS.md fails the section grep checks.

CKSPEC-AGENT-005.evidence: CLI is primary interface. All operations via task commands. --output json provides machine-readable responses. No protocol layer required.
CKSPEC-AGENT-005.violation_evidence: 'The CLI is the agent interface' is an architectural property (no separate protocol/daemon layer), verified by inspecting the design rather than a single artifact. Adding a non-CLI protocol layer is caught at architecture review.

CKSPEC-AGENT-006.evidence: ckeletin-go exposes a `catalog` command (cmd/catalog.go) that emits its command surface as the data of an OUT-002 success envelope (`catalog --output json`), derived from the live cobra command tree (RootCmd) - the same tree the parser uses - so the catalog cannot drift from the actual surface (anti-drift is structural, not tested-in). The shared schema types live in .ckeletin/pkg/catalog (Catalog/Command/Flag), matching the cross-implementation contract agreed with ckeletin-rust: the required core (long/required/takes_value) is always present and optional fields (short/description/default) are omitted when absent. Derivation and schema are verified by cmd/catalog_test.go and .ckeletin/pkg/catalog.

CKSPEC-AGENT-007.evidence: A project scaffolded from ckeletin-go never inherits ckeletin-go's own conformance truth. `task init` runs removeFrameworkOnlyArtifacts (.ckeletin/scripts/scaffold/helpers.go), which deletes the generated-evidence and upstream-only conformance artifacts that live outside the framework area — conformance-mapping.yaml, conformance-report.json, and test/conformance/ — so a derived project carries neither go's generated evidence nor its reference-only conformance prose (delete over stamp: a stale inherited report is worse than its absence). The framework area (.ckeletin/) is kept wholesale and replaced on update, with its upstream-only gate guarded INERT rather than stripped file-by-file: conform.sh is subject-gated (CKSPEC-ENF-009) — in a derived project (go.mod module is not the ckeletin-go reference) it declares its subject and exits 0 instead of hard-failing, so an inherited gate never blocks the derived project's release. The strip and the subject-gate no-op are verified end-to-end by test/integration/scaffold_init_test.go (TestScaffoldInit).
CKSPEC-AGENT-007.violation_evidence: Each check guards a distinct part of the mechanism. Removing the removeFrameworkOnlyArtifacts call from scaffold/main.go drops the strip entirely — check 1 (the main.go call-site grep) fails. Removing conformance-mapping.yaml from the artifact list in helpers.go makes init leak go's evidence into a derived project — check 2 (the helpers.go artifact grep) fails. Removing the subject-gate from conform.sh makes the inherited gate hard-fail in a derived project and block its release — check 3 fails, and TestScaffoldInit's "conformance gate subject-gated" subtest fails.

CKSPEC-ARCH-001.evidence: .go-arch-lint.yml defines four layers: commands (cmd/), business (internal/ping, docs, dev, progress, check), infrastructure (.ckeletin/pkg/*, internal/config/commands, internal/ui, internal/xdg). Entry layer is main.go (wiring only; size capped by the ARCH-006 check). task validate:layering passes.

CKSPEC-ARCH-002.evidence: go-arch-lint enforces directed dependencies. Business mayDependOn infrastructure only. Infrastructure mayDependOn nothing.

CKSPEC-ARCH-003.evidence: depOnAnyVendor:false enables vendor import checking. canUse rules restrict Cobra and pflag to commands layer only. Business and infrastructure cannot import them.

CKSPEC-ARCH-004.evidence: go-arch-lint enforces business packages cannot import each other. Each business package mayDependOn infrastructure only.

CKSPEC-ARCH-005.evidence: go-arch-lint enforces infrastructure mayDependOn nothing. Cannot import business or command layers.

CKSPEC-ARCH-006.evidence: main.go is a minimal wiring-only entry point (run() + error handling), capped at 60 lines by a conform check so the size claim cannot silently rot. validate-package-organization.sh verifies only main.go and main_test.go exist at root. validate-command-patterns.sh enforces the command-layer limits (run* functions ≤30-line target / 35 hard, command files ≤80 advisory).

CKSPEC-ARCH-007.evidence: validate-package-organization.sh checks all Go files are in designated directories (cmd/, internal/, pkg/, .ckeletin/). Only main.go/main_test.go at root.

CKSPEC-CL-002.evidence: CHANGELOG.md follows Keep a Changelog: the format is referenced in the header, and every change-group heading is one of the six standard categories (Added, Changed, Deprecated, Removed, Fixed, Security) — both grep-enforced. Reverse-chronological version order is verified at review/release, not by a check.
CKSPEC-CL-002.violation_evidence: Dropping the Keep a Changelog reference fails the first check; a non-standard change category heading (e.g. '### Misc') fails the second.

CKSPEC-CL-003.violation_evidence: A non-ISO-8601 release date fails the date-format grep check.

CKSPEC-CL-004.evidence: CHANGELOG.md states adherence to SemVer. Version numbers follow MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH throughout.
CKSPEC-CL-004.violation_evidence: A non-SemVer version header fails the grep check.

CKSPEC-CL-005.evidence: [Unreleased] section present at top of CHANGELOG.md.
CKSPEC-CL-005.violation_evidence: Removing the Unreleased section fails the grep check.

CKSPEC-CL-006.evidence: Changelog entries are human-written at user-relevant granularity. Not git log dumps.
CKSPEC-CL-006.violation_evidence: Distinguishing human-curated, user-relevant changelog prose from an auto-generated git-log dump is an editorial judgment no grep can make reliably. Enforced at review; a git-log-style dump is caught there.

CKSPEC-CL-007.evidence: Reference-style comparison links at bottom of CHANGELOG.md for all versions.
CKSPEC-CL-007.violation_evidence: Removing the reference-style compare links fails the grep check.

CKSPEC-ENF-001.evidence: ADR-014 enforcement audit: 12/14 ADRs fully automated, 1 partial (ADR-008 GoReleaser), 1 honor system (ADR-007 Bubble Tea). 23 automated checks run via task check. Enforcement coverage is verified by human review of the audit table, not a targeted check.
CKSPEC-ENF-001.violation_evidence: Meta-requirement: the project must prefer automated enforcement. Its satisfaction is the aggregate of the other requirements' enforcement levels (tracked in the ADR-014 audit table and surfaced by the conform report's automated-vs-analysis split), assessed at review rather than by one check.

CKSPEC-ENF-002.evidence: All ladder levels used. Linter: go-arch-lint + golangci-lint. SAST: semgrep rules (.semgrep.yml). Scripts: the validate-*.sh and check-*.sh suites in .ckeletin/scripts/ (counts are deliberately not hand-written here — they rot). CI: GitHub Actions. Honor system: ADR-007 (documented as last resort). The ladder's existence is verified by review, not a targeted check.
CKSPEC-ENF-002.violation_evidence: Removing the linter (.golangci.yml) or SAST (.semgrep.yml) config fails the test checks.

CKSPEC-ENF-003.evidence: ADR-014 enforcement audit table documents gaps explicitly. ADR-007: honor system. ADR-008: partial. Gap documentation is verified by human review of the audit table.
CKSPEC-ENF-003.violation_evidence: If ADR-014 stops documenting gaps, the grep check fails.

CKSPEC-ENF-004.evidence: ADR-014 contains living enforcement audit table tracking all 14 ADRs with enforcement mechanism, status, and gaps. Table existence verified by review.
CKSPEC-ENF-004.violation_evidence: If the ADR-014 audit table is removed, the grep check fails.

CKSPEC-ENF-005.evidence: task conform validates all 42 requirement IDs are present in conformance-mapping.yaml before running checks. Missing IDs cause the generator to fail with a non-zero exit code. The count is machine-verified by the ENF-008 drift guard, not hand-maintained.

CKSPEC-ENF-006.evidence: test/conformance/violation_test.go proves enforcement with one violation test per claim across the architecture (ARCH-001..007), output (OUT-001, OUT-005), testing (TEST-003), agent (AGENT-001, AGENT-002, AGENT-006), changelog (CL-001) and conformance-tooling (ENF-005, ENF-006, ENF-007, ENF-010 plus mapping-YAML/yq/report-sync integrity) claims. Requirements with honor-system enforcement are exempt; claims above honor-system without a violation test carry a written violation_evidence analysis instead (spec v0.4.0+), which TestViolation_ENF006_MissingViolationTestFlagged verifies for every enforcement level. Run with: go test -tags conformance ./test/conformance/...

CKSPEC-ENF-007.evidence: task conform automatically reports feedback signals when a requirement's enforcement_level is above honor-system without a violation test or violation evidence. Signals are structural, not volitional.
CKSPEC-ENF-007.violation_evidence: The violation test verifies the conform script contains feedback signal logic (string presence), not that it produces correct output — running conform.sh from within the conformance test suite causes infinite recursion. Behavioral proof: task conform output shows feedback signals when enforcement claims lack violation tests. File: .ckeletin/scripts/conform.sh (FEEDBACK_FILE logic). File: test/conformance/violation_test.go (TestViolation_ENF007).

CKSPEC-ENF-008.evidence: conform.sh fails if any requirement claimed met lacks both an automated check and a violation_evidence anchor — no unanchored met-claims. Anchors must also RESOLVE, not merely be present (#15, v0.11.0): a violation_test `file::symbol` anchor hard-fails when the file is missing or the named symbol is not defined in it — a renamed/removed test still cited by its old name is a dangling anchor and is rejected, so a stale anchor can never pass as proof. Met requirements are tallied as machine-anchored vs analysis-with-evidence and the split is printed. The same gate also catches prose count drift ("all N requirement").
CKSPEC-ENF-008.violation_evidence: The anchoring gate is conform.sh itself, so it is not cited as an in-mapping violation_test (that would re-run the generator from inside the suite — recursion). An external fixture test — run conform against a mapping with a deliberately dangling anchor, outside the suite — is the regression guard and is tracked as a follow-up. Behavioral proof (verified): stripping a met requirement's checks and violation_evidence makes task conform fail with "met but unanchored"; citing a renamed or nonexistent test symbol makes it fail with "dangling anchor ... not found"; a `file::` anchor with an empty symbol fails too. File: .ckeletin/scripts/conform.sh (ENF-008 anchoring gate + the automated-vs-analysis tally + the anchor-resolution check).

CKSPEC-ENF-009.evidence: .github/workflows/ci.yml defines a CKSPEC Conformance job that runs task conform; the release job declares needs: [test-matrix, build, conformance], so no release can publish while conformance fails. The gate declares its SUBJECT (CKSPEC-ENF-009 v0.9.0): conform.sh runs the full check in the ckeletin-go reference repo, but in a derived project (go.mod module is not the reference) it announces "subject: derived project ... no implementation-conformance to verify ... no-op by design" and exits 0 — so an inherited gate neither blocks the derived project's release nor silently passes as proven conformance. A scheduled run opens a deduplicated drift issue. Verified end-to-end via workflow_dispatch with the conformance job green, and by the subject-gate no-op subtest in test/integration/scaffold_init_test.go.
CKSPEC-ENF-009.violation_evidence: Enforcement is CI-structural — a job dependency in the release pipeline that a unit test cannot exercise. Proof is the workflow itself: the release job's needs: list includes conformance, so the release will not run while the conformance job fails. File: .github/workflows/ci.yml (conformance job + release needs).

CKSPEC-ENF-010.evidence: ckeletin-go publishes conformance-report.json — a deterministic, machine-readable projection of this mapping generated by gen-conformance-report.sh (yq extracts data, python canonicalizes formatting; no timestamp). task conform regenerates it in-memory and fails on drift (the report-sync guard), and the spec repo's aggregate_conformance.py consumes the published report instead of hand-authoring conformance/ckeletin-go.yaml.

CKSPEC-ENF-011.evidence: ckeletin-go's tree-walking scanners bound their traversal to the project's own sources and do not descend into nested repository or worktree checkouts. The shared exclusion lives in .ckeletin/scripts/lib/check-output.sh (skips .claude/worktrees/ and any directory carrying its own go.mod — a nested module is never part of this module's walk); the formatter (format-go.sh) shares that exclusion, and the architecture linter (.go-arch-lint.yml) excludes .claude/ as well. Surfaced by #123 (reported by daana-cli): a scanner descended into Claude Code's worktree directory and broke on stale nested module checkouts. Verified by test/integration/scanner_exclusion_test.go (TestScannersExcludeClaudeWorktrees): format check, format fix, layering validation, and — in CI, where semgrep is present — the SAST scan each ignore a stale worktree.
CKSPEC-ENF-011.violation_evidence: Removing the .claude/worktrees exclusion from check-output.sh (or the arch-lint config) lets the scanners descend into nested worktree checkouts again — the corresponding grep check fails, and TestScannersExcludeClaudeWorktrees fails (a stale worktree's unbuildable code is walked and flagged, or breaks the scan).

CKSPEC-OUT-001.evidence: ADR-013 defines 3-stream model. Logger writes console to stderr, file to JSON. internal/ui renders to stdout. validate-output-patterns.sh enforces separation.

CKSPEC-OUT-002.evidence: The global --output text|json flag (cmd/root.go PersistentPreRunE) activates JSON mode for every production command, each emitting the standard envelope to stdout: ping (ui.RenderSuccess), docs config (docs.GenerateJSON), config validate (ui.RenderValidationJSON), catalog, and --version (renderVersion). Stderr logging is silenced in JSON mode. Behavioral proof: the TestOutputJSON_* suite plus command-specific JSON tests execute each command with --output json and assert a single valid envelope. Documented exemptions: `completion` emits shell scripts whose only consumer is already a machine (the shell sources them; a JSON wrapper would break every `source <(app completion bash)` usage), and dev-only commands (check, dev *) are compiled out of production builds (//go:build dev) so they are not part of the released command surface.
CKSPEC-OUT-002.violation_evidence: Removing the JSON plumbing from the output package, the renderer, or any per-command wiring (docs config, config validate, catalog, --version) fails the per-file greps above. Behavioral coverage: cmd/output_json_test.go, cmd/docs_json_test.go, cmd/config_validate_json_test.go, cmd/catalog_test.go and cmd/version_output_test.go fail if any of those commands stops emitting a valid envelope under --output json.

CKSPEC-OUT-003.evidence: JSONEnvelope struct in .ckeletin/pkg/output/json.go emits the TOTAL envelope: all four top-level keys — status, command, data, error — carry no `omitempty`, so every key is present in every envelope and an absent value (data on error, error on success) is emitted as JSON null rather than omitted. The error object is itself total {code, message} (#40, v0.11.0): JSONError.Code is a *string with `json:"code"` (no omitempty), so an error with no classification still emits "code": null — present, never dropped. JSONResponder interface for custom payloads. Verified by unit tests, including TestRenderJSON_ErrorCodeNullWhenAbsent which asserts a code-less error serializes "code": null.
CKSPEC-OUT-003.violation_evidence: Removing the JSONEnvelope struct fails the first grep check; adding `omitempty` to any of the four envelope keys or to JSONError.Code (which would drop a key when absent instead of emitting null) fails the second.

CKSPEC-OUT-004.evidence: RenderSuccess and RenderError in internal/ui/renderer.go log.Debug() raw data to the audit log file while rendering formatted text to stdout; debug level reaches the file logger without polluting the console. Commands that stream generated artifacts shadow-log the operation's metadata rather than the full artifact: docs config records format, output file and content size (internal/docs/json.go, cmd/docs.go), and completion's shell scripts are cobra-generated artifacts. This metadata-not-artifact narrowing for generated artifacts is a documented project interpretation of OUT-004 (verbatim multi-KB artifacts would bloat the audit stream without adding evidence) — flagged upstream as spec feedback. Verified by unit tests.
CKSPEC-OUT-004.violation_evidence: Removing the shadow log.Debug from the renderer or from the docs JSON path fails the grep checks.

CKSPEC-OUT-005.evidence: validate-output-patterns.sh detects fmt.Print* and os.Stdout in internal/* (excluding tests). Business logic must use internal/ui for output. Documented scan exemptions: ui/ and logger/ ARE the rendering/audit layers, testutil/ is test plumbing, and progress/ touches os.Stdout only for terminal detection (IsInteractive(os.Stdout) in internal/progress/terminal.go) plus dev-build-only demo rendering — none of these emit business output directly.

CKSPEC-OUT-006.evidence: cmd/root.go composes the --version string as "<version>, commit <commit>, built at <date>, tree <state>" — all four OUT-006 fields including dirty-tree state. Uninjected builds degrade to explicit "unknown" (Version/Commit/Date default to versionUnknown, never empty). treeState() resolves clean/dirty/unknown from the Dirty ldflag (GoReleaser {{ .IsGitDirty }}), the git-describe "-dirty" suffix (Taskfile builds), or degrades to "unknown". --version honors --output json via renderVersion (versionData envelope with version/commit/date/tree).
CKSPEC-OUT-006.violation_evidence: Dropping any of the four fields from versionString(), the unknown-degradation defaults, or the JSON envelope path fails the greps and the behavioral tests the third check runs (TestVersionString_SurfacesAllFourFields, TestTreeState, TestBuildIdentityDefaults_UninjectedBuild, TestVersionFlag_JSONOutput in cmd/version_output_test.go), so the regression cannot land green.

CKSPEC-TEST-001.evidence: TDD mandatory per AGENTS.md and CLAUDE.md. Process discipline enforced through agent instructions, not tooling.
CKSPEC-TEST-001.violation_evidence: TDD is a temporal property — 'the failing test was written before the implementation' is not observable in the committed artifact, so no static check can decide it. Mandated by AGENTS.md/CLAUDE.md; a violation (implementation with no prior failing test) is caught at code review, not by tooling.

CKSPEC-TEST-002.evidence: check-coverage-project.sh enforces 85% minimum. Pre-push hook runs coverage check. Current coverage: 90.3%.
CKSPEC-TEST-002.violation_evidence: Destructive-to-test: reducing coverage below 85% to trigger the check would break the test suite. Enforcement mechanism is the script and its pre-push hook integration. File: .ckeletin/scripts/check-coverage-project.sh (MINIMUM_COVERAGE=85). File: .ckeletin/configs/lefthook.base.yml (pre-push runs coverage).

CKSPEC-TEST-003.evidence: Semgrep rule ckeletin-no-mock-frameworks detects gomock/mockery/ testify-mock imports in Go files. Violation test creates a file with a gomock import and verifies semgrep catches it.

CKSPEC-TEST-004.evidence: AGENTS.md mandates atomic commits. Process discipline enforced through agent instructions and code review.
CKSPEC-TEST-004.violation_evidence: Whether a commit atomically pairs a test with its implementation is a review judgment over commit contents, not a mechanically decidable property. Mandated by AGENTS.md and enforced at review.
